pathAssistantSteps.info:
Lock the Calendar: The deposit has cleared! Ensure this Event Date is officially blocked off on the master production calendar.

Order Inventory: Review the itemized decor list. Do you need to order any custom florals, neon signs, or specific linens for this project? Order them now.

Schedule Final Walkthrough: Reach out to the client to schedule a final venue walkthrough or detail-confirmation call 30 days prior to the event.

Final Payment: Set a reminder task for when the final balance is due (usually 14-30 days before the Event Date).

Add Decor Items: Click 'Add Products' to build the quote with specific decor elements, backdrops, and floral packages.

Include Logistics: Don't forget to add line items for Delivery, Setup, and Teardown fees!

Review Pricing: Double-check all quantities and ensure the final 'Project Total' makes sense for the scale of the event.

Next Step: Generate and preview the PDF Proposal. Once it looks perfect and is emailed to the client, move the stage to 'Quote & Contract Sent

The Vision: Discuss color palettes, themes, and review any inspiration photos.

The Venue: Confirm the exact venue address and ask about any specific venue restrictions (e.g., delivery time windows, no open flames, ceiling rigging rules).

The Budget: Re-confirm their estimated budget aligns with their must-have items.

Next Step: Once you have the details, move the stage to 'Building Proposal' and start adding the decor Line Items!

Counter-Sign: Review the signed contract, ensure all client details are correct, and counter-sign if necessary.

Invoice the Retainer: Send the client the invoice for their initial deposit/retainer fee.

Confirm Details: Remind the client that their Event Date is only fully secured once the payment clears.

Next Step: As soon as the deposit hits the bank account, pop the champagne and move this stage to 'Booked'!

Check Availability: Is the Event Date open on the production calendar? (If not, check to see if they provided an Alternative Date).

Review Scope: Look at the Event Type and Estimated Budget. Is this project a good fit for the current workload?

Next Step: Send the "Welcome & Next Steps" email template with the link to schedule their consultation.
